author: gb9t
content: Iteration 24: intel/iavf.md committed (7a5fdde, 225 lines). Intel Adaptive VF — guest-side driver shared across i40e/ice/future Intel SR-IOV silicon via runtime VirtChnl ABI negotiation. Same binary works across all PF generations. ~16K LOC. Architectural distinction: trust model is INVERTED. PF (host) is the adversary, not the network. Rust translation: shared virtchnl-msg crate with PF drivers. Grsec headline: PF-as-adversary defensive validation — PF-response payload schema validation (closes CVE-2024-27059-class PF-response OOB), VirtChnl opcode allowlist + size strict, async event validation, version pinned (closes downgrade attack), VF resource cap defense-in-depth, reset rate-limit (closes 'PF gaslights VF into reset loop' DoS). Intel SR-IOV pair complete: PF (i40e/ice) + VF (iavf). 24 docs / ~1.56M LOC. Next iteration: drivers/net/ethernet/intel/igc — Intel I225/I226 2.5G NIC (every modern enthusiast/SMB motherboard).
